title: Sidhhanath Temple, Kharsundi
tagline: Sidhhanath Temple, Kharsundi — spiritual town of Maharashtra

overview.short: Siddhanath is believed to be incarnation of Lord Shiva.
overview.description: Siddhanath is believed to be incarnation of Lord Shiva. Siddhanath is the patron god of Atpadi and adjacent regions, and is one of several regional protective (Kshetrapal) gods of Maharashtra. Historical temple of lord Siddhnath, a big temple built in 450 years ago. The God in this temple is reincarnation of Lord Shiva even called as NathBaba.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Sidhhanath Temple, Kharsundi?
faq.a: The best time to visit Sidhhanath Temple, Kharsundi is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Sidhhanath Temple, Kharsundi?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kolhapur Airport (91 km) and the nearest railway station is Solapur Junction (126 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Sidhhanath Temple, Kharsundi?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Sidhhanath Temple, Kharsundi at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Sidhhanath Temple, Kharsundi?
faq.a: Stays in Sidhhanath Temple, Kharsundi start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Sidhhanath Temple, Kharsundi?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kharsundi, Walwan, Balawdi, Bhoodsidhhanath, Bhood.
